headline: Rescued Crow Finds Second Chance With Feathered Foster Parent
meta_description: A malnourished jackdaw named Frank Sinatra is nursed back to health with help from an older bird who became his unlikely mentor.
summary_short: A starving baby crow found in Nottingham is recovering with an unexpected helper—an older fledgling who's teaching him to eat.
body:
When rescue workers found a tiny jackdaw in a field near Nottingham, England, he was barely clinging to life. Severely malnourished and nearly featherless, the six-week-old bird arrived at Brinsley Animal Rescue in what staff described as a "sorry state."

Volunteers named him Frank Sinatra after noticing his striking blue eyes. But the real turning point came when they placed him in recovery with an older fledgling—a decision that would transform his outlook.

The older bird did something remarkable: he became Frank's surrogate parent. Despite having only recently learned to feed himself, the fledgling began offering Frank worms and seeds, demonstrating the movements and patience his younger companion desperately needed. Videos show the pair perched together as Frank watches intently, copying his mentor's every move.

"At this stage of his growth, Frank would still be reliant on his parents for food," Jon Beresford, co-founder of the rescue centre, explained. "To our amazement, the older fledgling started feeding him."

The compassionate mentorship is accelerating Frank's recovery. Staff now hope he'll gain enough strength to move into an aviary where he can learn to fly and eventually return to the wild—carrying with him the unlikely bond that saved his life.
